title: piriqueta cistoides subsp. caroliniana
intro: Piriqueta cistoides subsp. caroliniana, also known as Carolina piriqueta, is a perennial herb native to the southeastern United States, characterized by its yellow flowers and edible roots. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating various health issues such as fever, cough, and skin conditions, due to its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Piriqueta cistoides subsp. caroliniana include its ability to alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Piriqueta cistoides subsp. caroliniana include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal benefits.
